summary: Merged effect-system matching PR that made `eflib`, new `eflib_alloc`, and `ef_061D` matching, while correcting effect allocator ownership, varargs prototypes, symbol/split boundaries, and related baselib particle/AppSRT declarations.

key_files.role: Main effect library rewrite/match; large cleanup of helpers, effect allocation/freeing, callbacks, assertions, varargs wrappers, and AppSRT generator attachment.
New 518-line matching implementation of `ef_80061D70`, a switch dispatcher for effect gfx IDs using `va_list` arguments.
New matching object containing `HSD_ObjAllocData efLib_AllocData` at BSS 0x80458EB0.
New public header exporting `efLib_AllocData` so non-eflib users do not include all of `eflib.h`.
Header guard cleanup, typed `_struct_efLib_80458EE0`, renamed `efLib_RemoveUserData`, corrected prototypes to `va_list`, `bool`, `u8`, and `u16` where evidenced.
Shared effect types cleanup: `Effect` flags changed to unsigned bytes, `ef_Symbols` typedef added, async objalloc struct moved here with static asserts.
Adjusted async effect code to use `ef_Symbols`, `va_list`, local `efAsync_AllocData`, and renamed fields such as `x0_gobj`.
Adjusted externs for `ef_Symbols`, `ef_80061D70(..., va_list)`, and `efLib_80458EE0` field naming.
Switched allocation limiter from `Effect_AllocData` to `efLib_AllocData` through the new allocation header.
Renamed `HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C` to `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` and changed it to return the created `HSD_psAppSRT*`.
Renamed local particle reference table uses from `ptclref` to `ptclref_804D0E5C`.
Reformatted/annotated `HSD_Particle` layout with explicit offsets, preserving size 0x98.
Added `melee/ef/eflib_alloc.c` split and adjusted eflib/ef_061D/efasync data and BSS boundaries.
Renamed functions/data, fixed allocator sizes, and changed many ef data symbols from global to local scope.
Marked `melee/ef/eflib_alloc.c`, `melee/ef/eflib.c`, and `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` as Matching in the ef library list.

what_changed: PR body states it matches `eflib`, new `eflib_alloc`, and `ef_061D`; `configure.py` changed those objects from NonMatching/new to Matching while leaving `efsync.c` and `efasync.c` NonMatching.

Added `src/melee/ef/eflib_alloc.c` and `.h` to own the effect allocator as `efLib_AllocData`; `splits.txt` gives it `.bss start:0x80458EB0 end:0x80458EDC`, and `symbols.txt` changes `Effect_AllocData` size from 0x30 to `efLib_AllocData` size 0x2C.
Updated `dballoc.c` to include `ef/eflib_alloc.h` and apply allocation limits to `efLib_AllocData` instead of the old `Effect_AllocData`.

Implemented `ef_80061D70(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* gobj, va_list vlist)` in `ef_061D.c`; it dispatches many gfx IDs, uses `EF061D_VA_ARG`, calls eflib creation helpers and `hsd_8039EFAC`, and drains `efLib_80458EE0` animation work before returning.

Reworked `eflib.c` from heavily placeholder/commented code into typed C using `Effect`, `ef_Symbols`, `_struct_efLib_80458EE0`, helper inlines/macros, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, and real effect allocator/free paths.
Renamed eflib symbols in config and headers: `Effect_RemoveUserData` became `efLib_RemoveUserData`, `fn_8005C904` became `efLib_RenderCallback`, and allocator references became `efLib_AllocData`.

Corrected varargs-related signatures: `efAsync_80063930` and `ef_80061D70` take `va_list`; `efLib_8005CD2C`, `efLib_8005CE48`, `efLib_8005CF40`, and `efLib_8005D044` now take `va_list` instead of `void*`.
Deleted `efasync.static.h`; moved its async objalloc struct/static assert into shared types and made the allocator a local `static HSD_ObjAllocData efAsync_AllocData` in `efasync.c`.
Adjusted `efasync` BSS naming from misleading `efSync_80458FA0` to `efAsync_AllocData`, including `HSD_ObjAlloc`, `HSD_ObjFree`, and `HSD_ObjAllocInit` call sites.
Renamed `HSD_PSAppSrt_803A425C` to `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` and changed it from void to returning the assigned `HSD_psAppSRT*`, matching the generator-side `psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin` pattern.
Renamed baselib particle static table `ptclref` to `ptclref_804D0E5C` across `particle.c`, `particle.static.h`, and `symbols.txt`.

Config data symbols were tightened: multiple efLib and ef_061D string/float symbols changed to local scope with corrected sizes/alignment, moving eflib `.data`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and async BSS ends.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code +0.62% / +24216 bytes, linked code +0.67% / +25828 bytes, 45 new matches, and full 100% matches for `main/melee/ef/ef_061D`, `main/melee/ef/eflib` data/items, and `main/melee/ef/eflib_alloc` BSS.
smart_moves: Split the allocator into `eflib_alloc.c` before `eflib.c` in both `splits.txt` and `configure.py`, which isolated BSS layout and let other modules depend only on the allocator header.
Renamed allocator ownership from generic `Effect_AllocData` to module-prefixed `efLib_AllocData`, reducing ambiguous cross-module includes and making `dballoc.c` dependency narrower.
Used shared typedefs and static asserts (`ef_Symbols`, `_struct_efLib_80458EE0`, async objalloc struct) instead of duplicate anonymous placeholder structs across eflib, efsync, and efasync.
Converted internal effect dispatch APIs to `va_list` rather than `void*`/ellipsis, matching the way sync/async spawners forward already-started varargs.
Left explicit matching aids in place where needed: `PAD_STACK`, hard-coded constants, copied `va_list` handling, and a `WALK_TO_ROOT` macro with a TODO explaining the inline did not yet keep a match.
Updated config symbol scopes from global to local for static ef strings/floats, which matters for exact data section size and for avoiding accidental public symbol assumptions.
Forward-declared selected functions in `eflib.c` with an explicit comment to avoid `sdata2` pollution, showing attention to data-section side effects from includes.
Made `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` return the created AppSRT pointer, enabling eflib helper code to both attach and immediately validate/setup the returned structure.
decomp_lessons: For effect-system matches, source changes and `config/GALE01/splits.txt`/`symbols.txt` changes must land together; object boundaries, BSS starts, local/global symbol scopes, and string sizes directly affect match status.
Allocator globals often deserve their own translation unit if the original binary layout has a standalone BSS slice; do not keep them inside the nearest logical C file if splits show otherwise.

When a function forwards variadic effect-spawn arguments, model internal helpers as `va_list` consumers and keep callers/headers consistent; mixing `void*`, ellipsis, and `va_list` can block matches or create unsafe ABI assumptions.

MWCC varargs and stack layout sometimes require non-idiomatic constructs such as typed `__va_arg` macros and `PAD_STACK`; preserve those locally and leave comments rather than over-cleaning a fragile match.
Static data should be marked local in `symbols.txt` when it is not genuinely exported; over-globalizing strings/floats can hide section-size and relocation problems.
If helper extraction changes codegen, use a macro/goto pattern temporarily and document the desired cleanup, as done around `WALK_TO_ROOT`.
Shared struct definitions with `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` are reusable matching infrastructure; they prevent later files from reintroducing anonymous placeholder structs with subtly wrong layout.
Renaming misleading cross-module symbols can reveal real ownership: `efSync_80458FA0` was actually async allocation data, and `Effect_AllocData` belonged to eflib allocation.
Header guards and includes were modernized away from address-based `GALE01_*` guards and broad `<placeholder.h>` includes; future decomp work should keep headers minimal and ownership-specific.
The PR evidence includes a WIP comment in `ef_061D.c` (`WIP CLEAN THIS UP AND SYNC`), so future cleanup should verify that later refactors preserve the exact varargs and animation-drain behavior.
naming_conventions: Use module prefix `efLib_` for eflib-owned functions/globals: `Effect_RemoveUserData` -> `efLib_RemoveUserData`, `Effect_AllocData` -> `efLib_AllocData`.
Name async allocator data by actual owner: `efAsync_AllocData`, not the misleading previous `efSync_80458FA0`.
Pair begin-style AppSRT helpers consistently: `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` mirrors `psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin` and returns `HSD_psAppSRT*`.
Use modern path-based header guards such as `MELEE_EF_EFLIB_H`, `MELEE_EF_EFASYNC_H`, and `MELEE_EF_EFALLOC_H` rather than address-derived `GALE01_*` guards.
Use shared descriptive typedefs when layout is understood enough: `_struct_efAsync_803C025C_0xC` became `ef_Symbols`.
For still-unknown locals, keep module/address names and mark local scope in symbols, e.g. `efLib_803BF7A0`, `ef_804D81D0`, and `ptclref_804D0E5C`.
Use field names that encode partial knowledge without overclaiming, such as `x0_gobj`, `x4_unk`, and `x6_unk` for `_struct_efLib_80458EE0`.
Keep public eflib APIs in `eflib.h`, but move standalone allocator exports to `eflib_alloc.h` when other modules only need allocation data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `EF061D_VA_ARG(t)` wraps `__va_arg(vlist_arg, _var_arg_typeof(t))` to pull typed arguments from a copied `va_list` in `ef_80061D70`.

`ef_80061D70` starts with `efLib_804D64E8 = 1` and `PAD_STACK(80)`, then drains pending `efLib_80458EE0` animation entries before returning; this ordering is part of the matched implementation evidence.
Hard-coded constants such as `-1.5707963267948966`, `1.5707963267948966`, and `1.5707964F` replaced more symbolic `M_PI_2` forms in matching-sensitive rotation code.
`efLib_8005D174` uses `PAD_STACK(28)` and a `WALK_TO_ROOT` macro with generated goto labels; the diff comment says an inline version still needed work to keep 100%.
The eflib source explicitly forward-declares `HSD_MtxGetScale` and `grLib_801C99C0` to avoid `sdata2` pollution from broader includes.
Repeated generator/AppSRT setup was centralized in `eflib_attach_gfx` and `efLib_EnsureGeneratorAppSRT_inline`, but the PR kept codegen-sensitive cases explicit where needed.
BSS sizes were corrected for allocators: `efLib_AllocData` size 0x2C at 0x80458EB0 and `efAsync_AllocData` size 0x2C at 0x80458FA0, instead of previous 0x30-sized placeholders.
Data matching required local scope and exact alignment for efLib/ef_061D string and float symbols in `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2`.

The PR used `HSD_ASSERTREPORT` with original messages like `Duplicate Free %08X`, `error no parent gobj!`, and `Over Anime Call` to preserve assert/report behavior while cleaning old OSReport/assert sequences.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or inline review comments are present in the slice.
PR body explicitly asked reviewers to point out anything missed or not kosher, suggesting the author expected style/process feedback, but none is captured in review comments.

decomp.dev bot report is the only substantive feedback-like record: it reported +24216 matched code bytes, 45 new matches, and one unmatched-item regression for `main/melee/ef/efasync` `efAsync_80063930` (-22 bytes, 98.61% to 98.46%).
The author later commented `womp womp, will fix tomorrow`; the slice does not provide enough evidence to tie that comment to a specific review request beyond its timing after the bot report.
PR was merged the same day by PsiLupan with no captured human review discussion.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs cleaning the `// WIP CLEAN THIS UP AND SYNC` comment in `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c`.
Search for later `efsync`/`efasync` matching PRs after #2274, especially any fix for the bot-reported `efAsync_80063930` regression.
Check whether `WALK_TO_ROOT` in `efLib_8005D174` was later converted from a macro/goto matching tactic into an inline without losing match.
Search for future renames of `efLib_8005D174`, `eflib_attach_gfx`, and the remaining `efLib_8005*` unknown functions once behavior was better understood.
Verify later handling of `efLib_804D64F0` declarations across `eflib.c`, `efsync.c`, and `ef_061D.c`, since this PR used matching-sensitive extern/array-style access patterns.
Look for later PRs that further type `ef_Symbols.unk8`, `_struct_efLib_80458EE0`, and `Effect` fields `x24` through `x2B`.
Search future baselib particle work around `ptclref_804D0E5C` and `psAddParticleAppSRT_begin` to see whether address-suffixed or behavior-based names won out.
